vitals.field_size: Approximately 1,000-1,200 riders historically (1,200 from 34 countries in 2024, nearly 1,000 in 2025); no official 2026 field cap published

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 64. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 61.

course_description.character: A capital-city gran fondo, not a mountain-climbing one. The long course runs 90.6 km with roughly 1,237 m of climbing (about 14 m/km), starting and finishing at Bilkent Center. The route opens with an urban climbing stretch through Hoşdere Caddesi, Simon Bolivar Caddesi and Turan Güneş Bulvarı past the Atakule tower, then heads out to the exposed rolling steppe south of the city through İncek, Tulumtaş, Halaçlı and Koparan before returning. Official course sheets identify road segments, not named climbs — there is no equivalent of a categorized mountain pass here.
course_description.signature_challenge: The city-centre climbing section past Atakule early in the route — the only sustained, tactically important climbing on an otherwise rolling, exposed course. Everything after it is a test of steppe headwinds and flat-out pace-holding, not gradient.
suffering_zones: Hoşdere Caddesi / Simon Bolivar Caddesi / Turan Güneş Bulvarı — the urban climbing opener past Atakule, where the route's real elevation gain is concentrated
İncek to Koparan — long, exposed rolling sections across Ankara's steppe, wide open to wind with no shelter
Tulumtaş–Halaçlı–Koparan — the outer loop that adds unglamorous connecting mileage before the return to Bilkent

logistics.lodging_strategy: Ankara offers standard urban accommodation options as a major capital city. Specific race-affiliated lodging information unavailable.

history.origin_story: First run in 2022 as an Ankara-based road gran fondo organized by 78 Event & Ajans, with 2026 marking the fifth edition. Title sponsorship (İş Bankası's Maximum Kart / Visa Maximum) and support from Ankara's municipal government and the Turkish Cycling Federation have grown the event from a local capital-city race into one of Turkey's better-attended amateur road gran fondos, though it remains a national rather than internationally prestigious event.

biased_opinion.verdict: A Solid Capital-City Fondo, Not a Climbing Test
biased_opinion.summary: Gran Fondo Başkent (Maximum Gran Fondo Başkent) is a legitimate Turkish amateur road event — its fifth edition runs in 2026 (founded 2022), on the Turkish Cycling Federation's official Gran Fondo calendar, and it has drawn roughly 1,000-1,200 riders per edition since its 2022 launch, with international representation growing from about 25 to 34 countries even as overall turnout has stayed flat rather than climbed. The database's prior 99.8 km figure never matched any documented edition: the actual long course is 90.6 km with 1,237 m of climbing, a moderately hilly urban-and-steppe route rather than a mountain gran fondo. Don't come to Ankara expecting sustained climbing — the defining terrain is a short urban climb past Atakule followed by long, exposed, rolling steppe roads south of the city.
strengths: On the Türkiye Bisiklet Federasyonu's official 2026 Gran Fondo calendar as a sanctioned amateur road race
Four completed editions since 2022 with a consistent ~1,000-1,200-rider turnout and international representation growing from roughly 25 to 34 countries
Municipal and federal government support (Ankara Governorate, Ankara Metropolitan Municipality) beyond a purely commercial production
A dual-distance format (short/long) plus paralympic and tandem categories broadens who can actually take part
weaknesses: Not a climbing gran fondo — 1,237 m over 90.6 km is moderately hilly at best; riders expecting Alpine-style suffering will be disappointed
Not a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier — Turkey's UCI-series slot belongs to Granfondo Antalya, not Başkent
No categorized or named climbs beyond city-street segments — the course sheet lists road names, not mountain passes
A national-level event with some international participation (per this database's prestige rubric), not yet an internationally prestigious destination fondo
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A well-run, federation-sanctioned capital-city fondo worth doing if you're in or near Ankara and want a moderately hilly, fast, urban-to-steppe test — just don't expect mountains, and don't confuse it with Turkey's actual UCI-series qualifier in Antalya.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ankara's growing capital-city fondo — a short urban climb, then long, exposed steppe roads, not a mountain test.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a well-organized, federation-backed Turkish road fondo with real international turnout and don't need sustained climbing. Skip it if you're chasing UCI Gran Fondo World Series points — that's Granfondo Antalya, not this race.
final_verdict.alternatives: For Turkey's actual UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier, look at Granfondo Antalya (November). For a genuinely mountainous Anatolian road event, look elsewhere in the Turkish federation's Gran Fondo calendar.

Ankara Provincial Culture/Tourism Directorate — Official Route Sheet
UCI Gran Fondo World Series — Official Calendar (Confirms Başkent Is Not a Qualifier)

videos.title: Maximum Gran Fondo Başkent 2025 Tamamlandı Yüzlerce Bisikletçi Ankara’da Buluştu
videos.description:
Türkiye’nin en prestijli amatör yol bisikleti yarışlarından Maximum Gran Fondo Başkent, 24 Ağustos Pazar günü gerçekleşti. Ankara’nın kalbinden başlayıp bozkır manzaralarına uzanan parkurlarda bisikletçiler hem zorlu hem de unutulmaz bir mücadele sergiledi. Gran Fondo Başkent'te kısa parkur erkeklerde Mithat Temizyürek (01:33:47), kadınlarda Aylin Yüce (01:40:27) finishi ilk geçen isimler oldu. Uzun parkurda erkeklerde Toygar Süner (02:25:53), kadınlarda ise Natalya Vallek (02:45:28) zafere ulaştı. 

Ankara Valiliği, Ankara Büyükşehir Belediyesi, Gençlik ve Spor Bakanlığı, Ankara Gençlik ve Spor İl Müdürlüğü ve Türkiye Bisiklet Federasyonu katkılarıyla, 78 Event organizasyonuyla düzenlenen yarış; sporun şehir kültürüyle buluştuğu, çevre bilincinin arttığı ve sağlıklı yaşamı teşvik eden bir festival havasında geçti. Türkiye İş Bankası’nın kredi kartı markası Maximum Kart’ın isim sponsorluğunu üstlendiği yarışa Škoda, MOSSO, SPX, Sports International, Bilkent Center, Bilkent Otel & Konferans Merkezi, Rova Teknoloji, Koru Hastanesi, Bilintur ve Tepe Emlak da destek verdi.

Bilkent Center önünden saat 08.00’da startı yaklaşık dört dakika süren Maximum Gran Fondo Başkent 2025, uzun ve kısa parkurların yanı sıra paralimpik ve tandem kategorilerinde de düzenlendi. Başlangıcın ardından sporcular, keyifli sürüşün yanında strateji ve dayanıklılığın ön planda olduğu zorlu etaplarda mücadele verdi. Teknik inişler, kontrollü tırmanışlar ve geniş düzlüklerden oluşan parkurun özellikle şehir merkezindeki tırmanış bölümü yarışın seyrini belirleyen en kritik nokta oldu. Katılımcılar, Ankara’nın simgesi Atakule önünden geçerek İncek, Tulumtaş, Halaçlı ve Koparan mahallelerini geride bıraktıktan sonra coşkulu kalabalıkların desteğiyle Bilkent’te finişe ulaştı.

Yüzlerce Pedal Ankara’da Buluştu

Gran Fondo Başkent 2025’e katılan farklı yaş gruplarından amatörler, kulüp sporcuları, şehir bisikletçileri ile paralimpik ve tandem kategorisindeki sporcular, sabahın erken saatlerinde Bilkent Center önünde start alarak Ankara’nın kalbinden kırsalına uzanan rotalarda mücadele etti.

Kısa parkur (56,6 km / 1.096 m tırmanış) ve uzun parkur (90,6 km / 1.237 m tırmanış) boyunca katılımcılar Atakule’nin simgesel görüntüsünden Tulumtaş Mahallesi’nin otantik atmosferine, Koparan’ın geniş tarım arazilerinden Angora Bulvarı’nın coşkulu seyirci desteğine uzanan unutulmaz bir deneyim yaşadılar.

Startı Ankara’nın Önde Gelen İsimleri Verdi

Gran Fondo Başkent 2025’in startı, Ankara Vali Yardımcısı Cem Afşin Akbay, Türkiye İş Bankası Bireysel Bankacılık Pazarlama Müdürü Çağlar İlter, Ankara Garnizon Komutanı Korgeneral Mahmut Kurumahmut, Ankara Merkez Komutanı Tümgeneral Ayhan Kalender, Ankara Başsavcısı Gökhan Karaköse, Ankara Kent Konseyi Başkanı ve Ankara Ticaret Odası Başkan Vekili Halil İbrahim Yılmaz, Ankara Gençlik ve Spor İl Müdürü Mustafa Çelik, ON İletişim Ajans Başkanı İpek Özgüden, Sports İnternational Genel Müdürü Sayın Hakan Öztürk, Bilkent Center Avm müdürü Esat Aydın, 78 Event Genel Müdürü Ömer Kafkas ve sponsor marka temsilcileri tarafından verildi.

Gran Fondo Başkent 2025’te Kazananlar Belli Oldu

Gran Fondo Başkent'te kısa parkur erkeklerde Mithat Temizyürek (01:33:47), kadınlarda Aylin Yüce (01:40:27) finishi ilk geçen isimler oldu. 

Uzun parkurda erkeklerde Toygar Süner (02:25:53), kadınlarda ise Natalya Vallek (02:45:28) zafere ulaştı.

rider_intel.search_text: The available transcript for Gran Fondo Başkent primarily consists of music and repeated mentions of heat, suggesting hot weather conditions during this road gran fondo in Ankara, Turkey (corrected from a prior mislabel as a 'gravel race' — this is a paved-road event). While the transcript lacks detailed course descriptions or rider commentary, the emphasis on heat indicates that temperature management may be a significant factor for participants in this Turkish gran fondo event.

eligibility.notes: CONFIRMED via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research, editorial wave 8. Fifth edition scheduled 23 August 2026, start/finish Bilkent Center, confirmed by the Turkish Cycling Federation's 2026 Gran Fondo calendar and July 2026 organizer/press coverage. Corrected this wave: distance_km 99.8->90.61 and elevation_m null->1237 (no credible source supported 99.8 km; the current official long-course page and the completed 2025 race both report 90.6 km/1,237 m). founded corrected to 2022 (first edition; 2026 is the fifth). Confirmed NOT a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier (Turkey's 2026 qualifier is Granfondo Antalya) despite being on the national federation calendar. SOL REVIEW CORRECTION (2026-07-24): a follow-up sol adversarial review found the initial draft overstated 'growing participation' — the 2022 inaugural edition already drew ~1,200 riders (hurriyetdailynews.com), so turnout has stayed roughly flat (not grown) while international representation grew from ~25 to 34 countries; wording corrected in biased_opinion/history accordingly.
